title: The Registration Commission
prose: For most of your housemates, this year is dangerous in the abstract — a bad feeling in the corridors, a professor to avoid. For {{name}}, it's closer than that. The Muggle-born Registration Commission wants proof of magical heritage that doesn't exist to give, and the wrong answer to the wrong question means Azkaban, or worse.

A quiet network of people who'd rather not be named helps where it can — forged paperwork, a borrowed family tree, a housemate willing to vouch for a cover story and mean it. It isn't safety, not really. It's just enough to keep going.

choices.label: Keep your head down, trust the people helping you, and keep going.
